paper_title: LLaMA: Open and Efficient Foundation Language Models

raw_extraction.innovation: Introduces efficient foundation language models trained on trillions of tokens using publicly available datasets, leveraging Chinchilla scaling laws and optimized architectures like pre-normalization, SwiGLU activation, and rotary positional embeddings. Demonstrates competitive performance with larger models despite lower parameter count
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: Common Crawl (67%), C4 (15%), Wikipedia (4.5%), Books (4.5%), ArXiv (2.5%), Stack Exchange (2%), Github (4.5%)
raw_extraction.application: General language tasks including chat, reasoning, code generation, and reading comprehension
raw_extraction.research_problem: Scalable, efficient, and open-source large language models
raw_extraction.paper_title: LLaMA: Open and Efficient Foundation Language Models

raw_extraction.innovation: Introduces efficient foundation language models trained on trillions of tokens using publicly available datasets, leveraging Chinchilla scaling laws and optimized architectures like pre-normalization, SwiGLU activation, and rotary positional embeddings. Demonstrates superior performance across benchmarks compared to GPT-3 despite being 10× smaller
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: Common Crawl (67%), C4 (15%), Wikipedia (4.5%), Books (4.5%), ArXiv (2.5%), Stack Exchange (2%), Github (4.5%)
raw_extraction.application: General language tasks including chat, reasoning, code generation, and reading comprehension
raw_extraction.research_problem: Efficient scaling of LLMs with open data
raw_extraction.paper_title: LLaMA: Open and Efficient Foundation Language Models

raw_extraction.innovation: Optimized architecture enhancements (pre-normalization, SwiGLU, RoPE) combined with extensive training on trillions of tokens from diverse public datasets, achieving strong performance comparable to much larger models
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: Common Crawl (67%), C4 (15%), Wikipedia (4.5%), Books (4.5%), ArXiv (2.5%), Stack Exchange (2%), Github (4.5%)
raw_extraction.application: Advanced language tasks including complex reasoning, code generation, and multilingual support
raw_extraction.research_problem: High-performance LLMs with cost-effective training/inference trade-offs
raw_extraction.paper_title: LLaMA: Open and Efficient Foundation Language Models

raw_extraction.innovation: Achieves competitive performance with top-tier models like PaLM-540B through optimized architecture (pre-normalization, SwiGLU, RoPE) and extensive training on trillions of tokens from diverse public datasets, demonstrating scalability benefits beyond simple parameter count increases
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: Common Crawl (67%), C4 (15%), Wikipedia (4.5%), Books (4.5%), ArXiv (2.5%), Stack Exchange (2%), Github (4.5%)
raw_extraction.application: Complex language understanding, reasoning, and multilingual applications
raw_extraction.research_problem: Balancing model size, performance, and computational efficiency
raw_extraction.paper_title: LLaMA: Open and Efficient Foundation Language Models

raw_extraction.innovation: Introduced open-source, efficient foundation language models leveraging Transformer decoder architecture with next-token prediction objectives. Emphasizes cost-effectiveness and accessibility through open licensing
raw_extraction.application: General-purpose natural language processing, text generation, and multitask learning
raw_extraction.license: Open-source (details not fully specified in provided excerpt)
raw_extraction.research_problem: Efficiently scaling large language models while maintaining performance
raw_extraction.paper_title: LLaMA: Open and Efficient Foundation Language Models

raw_extraction.innovation: Introduced larger model variant (65B parameters) demonstrating improved capabilities across various domains while maintaining efficiency through open architecture principles
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: Large-scale text corpus (likely similar to base model)
raw_extraction.application: Advanced natural language understanding, complex reasoning tasks, and specialized domain applications
raw_extraction.research_problem: Scalability and performance improvements in large language models
raw_extraction.paper_title: LLaMA: Open and Efficient Foundation Language Models

raw_extraction.innovation: Introduces Chinchilla sampling strategy—a more data-efficient training methodology—to optimize model performance while maintaining open-source accessibility. This variant specifically applies this technique to achieve superior efficiency with 65B parameters
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: Optimized Chinchilla sampling strategy applied to custom filtered web text corpus
raw_extraction.application: Advanced language understanding and generation, specialized research applications
raw_extraction.research_problem: Data-efficient large language model scaling with open access
raw_extraction.extension: Applied Chinchilla sampling strategy to improve training efficiency relative to standard language modeling approaches
raw_extraction.paper_title: LLaMA: Open and Efficient Foundation Language Models
